---
title: "Path Planning"
---

This page is a high-level map of path planning methods used in robotics.

## Definition and Goal

Path planning computes a feasible route from a start state to a goal state while satisfying constraints such as collision avoidance, kinematic limits, and environment changes.

## Taxonomy at a Glance

Two useful ways to categorize methods are:

- Scope: global vs local planning
- Representation: grid/graph search, sampling-based, and optimization-based

## Method Families

The lists below are ordered in a sensible study sequence: start from simpler baseline methods, then move to faster variants, replanning methods, and more specialized planners.

### Grid and Graph Search

- Grid-Based Planning Overview. [Detail](details/grid-graph/grid-based-overview.qmd)
- BFS: Unweighted graph search for shortest path in number of edges. [Detail](details/grid-graph/bfs.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#bfs)
- Dijkstra: Weighted graph search that guarantees optimal cost-to-go. [Detail](details/grid-graph/dijkstra.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#dijkstra)
- A*: Heuristic search balancing speed and solution quality. [Detail](details/grid-graph/a-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#a-star)
- Theta*: Any-angle extension of A* that reduces grid-constrained zig-zag paths. [Detail](details/grid-graph/theta-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#theta-star)
- JPS (Jump Point Search): Grid-search acceleration technique that prunes symmetric expansions on uniform-cost maps. [Detail](details/grid-graph/jps.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#jps)
- D*: Incremental graph replanning for changing maps. [Detail](details/grid-graph/d-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#d-star)
- D* Lite: Simplified and efficient incremental replanning based on Lifelong Planning A*. [Detail](details/grid-graph/d-star-lite.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#d-star-lite)
- Anytime A*: Produces a fast initial solution and improves it as more time is available. [Detail](details/grid-graph/anytime-a-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#anytime-a-star)
- Anytime D*: Anytime replanning variant for dynamic environments with bounded improvement over time. [Detail](details/grid-graph/anytime-d-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#anytime-d-star)
- Hybrid A*: A* variant with continuous heading and vehicle-feasible motion primitives. [Detail](details/grid-graph/hybrid-a-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#hybrid-a-star)

### Sampling-Based Planning

- Sampling-Based Planning Overview. [Detail](details/sample_based/sample-based-overview.qmd)
- PRM: Builds a roadmap of collision-free samples for multi-query planning. [Detail](details/sample_based/prm.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#prm)
- RRT: Rapidly explores feasible space for fast first-solution discovery. [Detail](details/sample_based/rrt.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#rrt)
- RRT*: RRT variant that rewires for asymptotic optimality. [Detail](details/sample_based/rrt-star.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#rrt-star)

### Local Planning and Trajectory Methods

- Local Planning Overview. [Detail](details/local-planning/local-planning-overview.qmd)
- DWA (Dynamic Window Approach): Evaluates short-horizon velocity commands and selects a safe local motion. [Detail](details/local-planning/dwa.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#dwa)
- TEB (Timed Elastic Band): Optimizes a local time-parameterized trajectory under obstacle and kinematic constraints. [Detail](details/local-planning/teb.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#teb)
- Frenet-frame planners: Generate trajectories in road-aligned Frenet coordinates, common in structured driving scenarios. [Detail](details/local-planning/frenet-frame-planners.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#frenet-frame-planners)

### Optimization and Predictive Methods

- MPC (Model Predictive Control): Solves a constrained predictive optimization problem at each control step. [Detail](details/optimization/mpc.qmd) | [Demo](demos.qmd#mpc)

## Additional Methods to Consider

This list is a coverage checklist for future expansion:

- State lattice planning: Structured motion primitives for nonholonomic vehicles.
- Kinodynamic RRT/RRT*: Sampling with dynamic feasibility.
- CHOMP/STOMP: Optimization-based trajectory generation.

## Suggested Study Order

If you want to study these methods progressively, this order builds the main ideas with fewer jumps:

1. BFS -> Dijkstra -> A*
2. Theta* and JPS
3. D* and D* Lite
4. Anytime A* and Anytime D*
5. Hybrid A*
6. PRM -> RRT -> RRT*
7. DWA -> TEB -> Frenet-frame planners
8. MPC

## Method Comparison (Placeholder)

| Method | Category | Horizon | Dynamics | Optimality | Speed | Typical Role |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| BFS | graph | Global | Basic | Low | Fast | Baseline graph search |
| Dijkstra | graph | Global | Basic | High | Medium | Cost-optimal graph planning |
| A* | graph | Global | Basic | Good | Fast | Heuristic global planning |
| Theta* | graph | Global | Basic | Good | Fast | Any-angle global planning |
| JPS | graph | Global | Basic | Good | Very fast | Accelerated grid search |
| Anytime A* | graph | Global | Basic | Improving | Fast/Anytime | Time-bounded heuristic planning |
| D* | graph | Global/Replan | Basic | Good | Medium | Dynamic map replanning |
| D* Lite | graph | Global/Replan | Basic | Good | Medium/Fast | Incremental replanning |
| Anytime D* | graph | Global/Replan | Basic | Improving | Medium | Anytime dynamic replanning |
| Hybrid A* | graph | Global | Moderate | Good | Medium | Kinematically-feasible global planning |
| PRM | sample | Global | Moderate | Moderate | Medium | Multi-query roadmap planning |
| RRT | sample | Global | Moderate | Low | Fast | Feasible path discovery |
| RRT* | sample | Global | Moderate | High | Slower | Asymptotically optimal sampling |
| DWA | local | Short | Basic | Low | Fast | Local planning |
| TEB | local | Medium | Good | Moderate | Medium | Local trajectory optimization |
| Frenet-frame planners | local | Medium | Good | Moderate | Medium | Structured-road trajectory generation |
| MPC | optimization | Predictive | Excellent | High | Slower | Advanced control |

## Algorithm Selection Guide (Placeholder)

| Scenario | Candidate Methods | Why | Main Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|---|
| Static grid map | A*, Dijkstra | Reliable and interpretable | Grid resolution affects quality |
| Unknown or changing map | D*, D* Lite | Incremental replanning | More implementation complexity |
| Car-like nonholonomic robot | Hybrid A* | Respects turning constraints | Higher compute than plain A* |
| Dense local obstacle field | DWA, TEB | Fast local maneuvering | Local minima and parameter sensitivity |
| Constrained trajectory tracking | MPC | Explicit constraint handling | Model and tuning effort |

## Practical Planning Stack (Placeholder)

Most systems combine methods rather than using one planner only:

1. Global planner proposes a long-range route.
2. Local planner adapts motion around near obstacles.
3. Controller tracks the selected trajectory.

## Detailed Pages and Demos

- Use this page as the summary index.
- Place detailed explanations in focused subpages.
- Use the demos page for runnable examples and comparisons.
